Chr.Avro.Cli 10.13.0+26a5751b039bfb1a5d55fc0ed9018eb8e4ae90f5
Copyright (C) 2026 C.H. Robinson

  create           Create an Avro schema for a .NET type.

  generate         Generate C# code for a schema from the Schema Registry or
                   stdin.

  registry-get     Retrieve a schema from the Schema Registry.

  registry-test    Verify that a .NET type is compatible with a schema in the
                   Schema Registry.

  help             Display more information on a specific command.

  version          Display version information.

result.stderr:
Chr.Avro.Cli 10.13.0+26a5751b039bfb1a5d55fc0ed9018eb8e4ae90f5
Copyright (C) 2026 C.H. Robinson
USAGE:
Create a schema for a built-in type:
  dotnet avro create --type System.DateTime
Create a schema for a type in a compiled assembly:
  dotnet avro create --assembly ./out/Example.Models.dll --type
  Example.Models.ExampleModel
Create a schema for a type in a compiled assembly with dependencies:
  dotnet avro create --assembly System.Text.Json ./out/Example.Models.dll --type
  Example.Models.ExampleModel

  -a, --assembly           The name of or path to an assembly to load (multiple
                           space-separated values accepted).

  --enum-behavior          The type of schema that enum types should be
                           represented by. Options are "symbolic" (generate an
                           "enum" schema; the default behavior), "integral"
                           (generate an "int" or "long" schema based on the
                           underlying type; the behavior for all flag enums),
                           and "nominal" (generate a "string" schema).

  --nullable-references    (Default: Annotated) Which reference types should be
                           represented with nullable union schemas. Options are
                           "annotated" (use nullable annotations if available;
                           the default behavior), "none", and "all".

  --temporal-behavior      Whether timestamps should be represented with
                           "string" schemas (ISO 8601) or "long" schemas
                           (timestamp logical types). Options are "iso8601",
                           "epochmilliseconds", and "epochmicroseconds".

  -t, --type               Required. The type to build a schema for.

  --help                   Display this help screen.

  --version                Display version information.

result.stderr:
Chr.Avro.Cli 10.13.0+26a5751b039bfb1a5d55fc0ed9018eb8e4ae90f5
Copyright (C) 2026 C.H. Robinson
USAGE:
Generate code for a schema by ID:
  dotnet avro generate --id 120 --registry-url http://registry:8081
Generate code for a schema by ID, connecting to the Schema Registry using basic
auth:
  dotnet avro generate --registry-config
  schema.registry.basic.auth.user.info=exampleuser:password --id 120
  --registry-url http://registry:8081

  -c, --registry-config            Configuration options to provide to the
                                   registry client (multiple space-separated
                                   key=value pairs accepted).

  -r, --registry-url               The URL of the schema registry.

  -i, --id                         If a subject/version is not specified, the ID
                                   of the schema.

  -s, --subject                    If an ID is not specified, the subject of the
                                   schema.

  --component-model-annotations    Whether to emit component model annotations
                                   for record and enum descriptions.

  --nullable-references            Whether reference types selected for nullable
                                   record fields should be annotated as
                                   nullable.

  -v, --version                    The version of the schema.

  --help                           Display this help screen.

  --version                        Display version information.

                           client (multiple space-separated key=value pairs
                           accepted).

  -r, --registry-url       Required. The URL of the schema registry.

  -i, --id                 Required. If a subject/version is not specified, the
                           ID of the schema.

  -s, --subject            Required. If an ID is not specified, the subject of
                           the schema.

  -v, --version            The version of the schema.

  --help                   Display this help screen.

  --version                Display version information.

Chr.Avro.Cli 10.13.0+26a5751b039bfb1a5d55fc0ed9018eb8e4ae90f5
Copyright (C) 2026 C.H. Robinson
USAGE:
Test that a type works with the latest version of a subject:
  dotnet avro registry-test --assembly ./out/Example.Models.dll --registry-url
  http://registry:8081 --subject example_subject --type
  Example.Models.ExampleModel

  -a, --assembly           The name of or path to an assembly to load (multiple
                           space-separated values accepted).

  -t, --type               Required. The type to test.

  -c, --registry-config    Configuration options to provide to the registry
                           client (multiple space-separated key=value pairs
                           accepted).

  -r, --registry-url       Required. The URL of the schema registry.

  -i, --id                 Required. If a subject/version is not specified, the
                           ID of the target schema.

  -s, --subject            Required. If an ID is not specified, the subject of
                           the target schema.

  -v, --version            The version of the target schema.

  --help                   Display this help screen.

  --version                Display version information.
